title: nephrodium polymorphum var. simonsii
intro: Nephrodium polymorphum var. simonsii, also known as the simons' maidenhair fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are due to the presence of certain compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The simons' maidenhair fern has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and is said to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. Herbal preparations of the simons' maidenhair fern can be made by infusing the dried fronds in hot water to create a tea or tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments.
